content: Comparative analyses of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM, RND) for reinforcement learning can be reframed as a benchmark for how an agent's self-model of its own epistemic limits shapes long-term exploratory performance, rather than just measuring how much reward an agent gains from novelty. This reframe reveals that existing comparisons between curiosity-driven agents often misattribute performance differences to intrinsic reward design when they actually stem from differences in how each agent encodes long-term memory of already explored states.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
